content:
# Final Review Aggregate

**Summary:** Final review accepted 4 amendment(s); restart required.
**Restart Required:** true
**Force Completed:** false
**Total Reviewers:** 2
**Unique Amendments:** 4
**Final Review Restarts:** 3/25
**Effective Min Reviewers:** 1
**Exhausted Count:** 1

## Accepted

- `fr-3-e76b2f84`
- `fr-3-6bfeb81e`
- `fr-3-8f748c9e`
- `fr-3-f08e0ba4`

## Rejected

None.

## Disputed

None.

## Final Accepted Amendments

### `fr-3-e76b2f84`

finding_summary: The new bead-export path deserializes `br show --json` into `BeadDetail`, but the real `br` CLI returns a one-element array. `materialize_beads()` does this on every create/reuse path (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1784-1802`, `2014-2023`), while `BrCommand::show()` always emits `br show <id> --json` (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:352-355`) and `exec_json()` blindly parses into the requested type (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:769-779`). I verified locally in this repo that `br show ralph-burning-9ni.4.6 --json | jq -r 'type'` returns `array`. As written, `milestone export-beads` will raise `BrParseError` the first time it tries to resolve a created bead or inspect an existing one. The new tests miss this because the helpers at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5235-5300` mock `show` as an object rather than the real array payload.

### `fr-3-6bfeb81e`

finding_summary: The idempotency/reuse logic only treats `open` and `in_progress` beads as reusable, and converts any closed/deferred milestone-scoped title match into a hard failure. `summary_matches_materialized_bead_except_role()` filters matches to `Open | InProgress` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1904-1908`), then `find_existing_materialized_bead()` returns an error when no eligible match remains (`1968-1981`) or when a valid match coexists with a non-active historical duplicate (`1984-2007`). In practice, rerunning `milestone export-beads` after some previously exported beads have been completed or deferred will fail instead of skipping those existing beads and exporting newly added work, which breaks the command's expected idempotent/incremental behavior.

### `fr-3-8f748c9e`

BrMutationAdapter::create_bead in src/adapters/br_process.rs:1333-1360 builds `--label=<value>` for each label via `cmd.kv("label", label.as_str())` (line 1343). `br 0.1.35` rejects that argument at the parser: running `br create --label=alpha --dry-run` returns `error: unexpected argument '--label' found; tip: a similar argument exists: '--labels'`. The correct invocations are either `--labels=a,b,c` (comma-separated) or `-l a -l b -l c` (repeated short flag). Because the new materialize_beads path in src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs always injects at least a `milestone:<id>` label on every created bead (see materialized_bead_labels), every real-world run of `milestone export-beads <id>` fails at the first create_bead call — no beads created, no dependencies wired, no journal event emitted. The mock-based tests in service.rs don't catch this because MockBrAdapter short-circuits the subprocess entirely. Fix options: (a) change `cmd.kv("label", ...)` in a new loop to `cmd.flag_value("-l", label.as_str())` or similar, emitting `-l <label>` per label; or (b) collect labels and emit a single `--labels=<a>,<b>,<c>` key-value (being careful to forbid commas inside any individual label). Option (a) preserves the existing one-flag-per-label shape; option (b) matches br's native preferred form. Either resolves the blocker. The existing internal test at br_process.rs:2231-2232 should also be updated to assert the new flag shape.

### `fr-3-f08e0ba4`

find_existing_materialized_bead in src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1940-1982 keys existing beads only on normalized title. When a bundle contains two proposals with identical titles (bundle validation permits duplicate titles when both carry explicit bead_ids — see bundle validation logic and canonical_export_proposal_id), a re-export sees two milestone-scoped beads with the same title+type and bails with `multiple milestone-scoped beads already match title '<t>' and type '<bt>'` at line 1963. This silently breaks the idempotency guarantee for a legitimate bundle shape: the first export succeeds, creates two beads with identical titles and identical `milestone:<id>` labels but distinct br-assigned IDs; a subsequent export (e.g. after a crash between dep resolution passes, or a deliberate retry) cannot reconcile which existing bead corresponds to which proposal. The matcher would need to consult the canonical proposal_id (via comment body, a dedicated label like `milestone-proposal:<canonical_id>`, or a stable description marker) to disambiguate. Until fixed, bundle authors must avoid same-title proposals, which isn't enforced at validation time.
